title: Fermented Tender Egyptian Prunes Tagine
description: A rich and aromatic brunch dish featuring the sweet and tender flavors of prunes, coconut cream, and warm spices. This vegetarian-friendly recipe is perfect for a leisurely weekend morning.
instructions: In a large bowl, combine prunes and yeast, and add 1 cup of water. Stir to dissolve yeast, then set aside for 10 minutes.
In a large saucepan, heat coconut oil over medium heat. Add onion, garlic, and ginger, and cook until fragrant, about 5 minutes.
Add the cumin, cinnamon, turmeric, and salt to the saucepan, and cook for 1 minute.
Add the prunes and yeast mixture to the saucepan, and stir to combine.
Pour in the coconut cream, and stir to combine.
Bring the mixture to a boil, then reduce the heat to low and simmer, uncovered, for 20 minutes.
Transfer the tagine to a serving dish, and garnish with chopped fresh herbs, if desired.
Serve warm, or at room temperature.
Refrigerate any leftovers, and allow to ferment for 24 hours before serving.
Before serving, stir in any accumulated liquid from the refrigerator.
